title: [lichen_link_rx.c:232-236] SHA state not zeroed on all paths
description:
Gap: sha_state and hash buffers are not zeroed after use in the EUI-64 derivation code.

What: Lines 229-236 create a tc_sha256_state_struct on the stack and a 32-byte hash buffer. Neither is zeroed after use. The lichen_sha256() helper in lichen_util.c explicitly zeros the state with secure_zero(&state, sizeof(state)), but lichen_link_rx.c does not follow this pattern.

Why it matters: While the hash of a public key is not secret, the sha_state struct contains internal hash state that could aid cryptanalysis if leaked. Defense-in-depth suggests zeroing cryptographic state consistently, matching the pattern in lichen_util.c.
close_reason: File is in lichen/ west workspace (external Zephyr module), not bridge-zephyr application code. The west workspace code at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/ is managed separately from the main firmware code at firmware/bridge-zephyr/. Defense-in-depth fix would need to be applied upstream in the west module.
